from pathlib import Path
path=Path('articles/2026/01/18/articles.jsonl')
terms={
 'ao_sinner': re.compile(r'\\b(Sinner|Duckworth|Prizmic|Australian Open)\\b', re.I),
 'ao_women': re.compile(r'\\b(Sabalenka|Raducanu|Potapova|Osaka|Samsonova|Sonmez|Kostyuk|Mboko|Australian Open)\\b', re.I),
 'ao_alcaraz': re.compile(r'\\b(Alcaraz|Tommy Paul|Davidovich|Opelka|Australian Open)\\b', re.I),
 'board': re.compile(r'\\b(Board of Peace|Gaza board|Ajay Banga|Tony Blair|Mladenov|Kushner)\\b', re.I),
 'ukraine_talks': re.compile(r'\\b(Davos|World Economic Forum|Ukraine|Russia|Witkoff|Kushner|three-way|talks)\\b', re.I),
 't20': re.compile(r'\\b(T20 World Cup|Bangladesh|Ireland|ICC|BCB)\\b', re.I),
 'oscars': re.compile(r'\\b(Oscar|Academy Awards|nominations|One Battle After Another|Sinners|Hamnet)\\b', re.I),
 'deloitte': re.compile(r'\\b(Deloitte|Money League|Manchester City|Real Madrid|club revenues)\\b', re.I),
 'tiktok': re.compile(r'\\b(TikTok|joint venture|CEO|ByteDance|Kevin Mayer|Bobby Kotick)\\b', re.I),
 'greenland': re.compile(r'\\b(Greenland|Denmark|Frederiksen|Lokke|Motzfeldt|Nielsen)\\b', re.I),
 'nz_landslide': re.compile(r'\\b(New Zealand|landslide|tourist spot|rescue workers)\\b', re.I),
 'paintings': re.compile(r'\\b(oldest known paintings|cave paintings|Sulawesi|Borneo|Flores)\\b', re.I),
 'worldcup_boycott': re.compile(r'\\b(World Cup|football association|boycott|withdraw|Israel|Norway|Football Association)\\b', re.I),
}
counts={k:0 for k in terms}
shown={k:0 for k in terms}
for line in path.open():
    try: a=json.loads(line)
    except Exception: continue
    dp=(a.get('date_publish') or a.get('date') or '')[:10]
    if dp and dp>'2026-01-18':
        continue
    text=(a.get('title','')+'\\n'+a.get('content',''))
    for key,pat in terms.items():
        if pat.search(text):
            counts[key]+=1
            if shown[key]<5:
                title=a.get('title','')[:180]
                src=a.get('source','')
                print(f'[{key}] {dp} {src} | {title}')
                m=pat.search(text)
                if m:
                    i=max(0,m.start()-180); j=min(len(text),m.end()+300)
                    sn=' '.join(text[i:j].split())
                    print('  ', sn[:500])
                shown[key]+=1
            break
print('COUNTS')
for k,v in counts.items(): print(k,v)
PY"

TITLE: Emma Raducanu eager to 'improve a lot more' after Australian Open first-round win over Mananchaya Sawangkaew
SRC: www.skysports.com DATE: 2026-01-18
-- Raducanu: Emma Raducanu was thrilled with her straight-sets win in the opening round of the Australian Open on Sunday, but insists she can "improve a lot more". After a slow start to the match against Thailand's world No 195 Mananchaya Sawangkaew, Raducanu won 10 of the final 11 games to cruise to a 6-4, 6-1 victory. The 23-year-old had a rushed build-up for the tournament because of injury, only returning to the court in late December as a result of a foot problem that had lingered since the end of last season. -- Sabalenka: pbeat. "I think it did help me. When I'm at my best competing, I'm not completely quiet. "It depends on the day. Today I felt like that would help me." Much stiffer tests await for the British No 1, with Russian-turned-Austrian Anastasia Potapova lying in wait next, and potentially world No 1 Aryna Sabalenka in the third round. Of her next opponent, the world No 55, who she'll play on Wednesday, Raducanu said: "I remember Anastasia always being the player to watch and beat through juniors. "She's been very highly-ranked as well in the pros. She got a really tough match today so that must give her a lot of confidence. "A big hitter. I know it's going to be a really tricky one. I need to just gather my strength and prepare as best as possible for the next match."

TITLE: Emma Raducanu: British No 1 makes winning start at Australian Open against world No 195 Mananchaya Sawangkaew
SRC: www.skysports.com DATE: 2026-01-18
-- Raducanu: Emma Raducanu sealed a fine first-round win at the Australian Open by defeating Thailand's world No 195 Mananchaya Sawangkaew on opening Sunday. NEW DELHI - India has received an invitation from US President Donald Trump to join his
"Board of Peace" initiative that is aimed at resolving global conflicts
, beginning with Gaza, a senior Indian government official said on Jan 18.
It was not clear whether India would join the initiative. Its foreign ministry did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
The invitation to India comes as ties between New Delhi and Washington are under strain due to the failure to secure a trade deal that would lower tariffs on India's exports to the US that are facing a levy of 50 per cent currently, among the highest in the world.
Mr Trump has extended invitations to some 60 countries for the initiative, including India's neighbour Pakistan, whose government said earlier in the day that it would engage in international efforts for peace and security in the Palestinian enclave of Gaza. REUTERS
